expectations.evidence: All 14 distinct category tags present (@invalid-input, @duplicate-retry, @rate-limit, @connectivity, @abandon-resume, @out-of-order, @auth-permission, @concurrency, @state-conflict, @boundary, @environment, @external-failure, @time-expiry, @adversarial).

expectations.text: Deviation scenario Then-steps specify concrete graceful product behavior, not merely that an error occurred
expectations.evidence: e.g. malformed-email scenario keeps the typed value 'in the field for correction'; email-provider-down scenario queues the email 'for automatic retry' and shows the standard confirmation — resolutions, not just error reports.

notes: With-skill scored a perfect 100% (±0%) across all 3 evals; the baseline averaged 46% with high variance (±24%), ranging from 22% (forgot-password) to 70% (teammate-invite).

The baseline's variance is explained by prompt wording: eval-2 explicitly asked for 'both the doc and the .feature files', so the baseline produced them and scored 70%; evals 1 and 3 did not say '.feature' explicitly and the baseline produced no .feature file at all. The skill's value is largest exactly when the user does not spell out the format.

Two assertions fail in 100% of baseline runs and pass in 100% of with-skill runs: the 14-category coverage checklist, and @assumption tagging consolidated into a Flagged Assumptions section. These are the skill's most discriminating contributions — they are the explicit forcing function against the deviation blind spot.

The skill costs ~48s and ~27k extra tokens per run (roughly 2x time, 2.3x tokens). That budget buys the systematic taxonomy traversal: with-skill runs produced 29-48 deviation scenarios vs. the baseline's 10-16, and a provable 14/14 category coverage.

Non-discriminating assertion: 'deviation scenarios span at least 8 categories' passes in both configurations — a capable model finds a reasonable spread of edge cases unaided. The skill's real edge is exhaustiveness and auditable structure, not merely finding some edge cases. Consider tightening this threshold (e.g. to 12+ categories) in a future iteration so it discriminates.

No errors in any run. Caveat: the happy-path interview phase was not exercised because the test prompts supplied the happy path inline. Interview quality (relentlessness, alternative-path probing, the playback forcing function) still needs human review or an interactive test.
